title: check_sycophancy
description: Detect (a) over-validation patterns in a candidate LLM response or (b) repeated reassurance-seeking in recent user messages on the same decision. Returns the matched pattern, a confidence score, a counter-prompt for the calling skill to optionally surface to the model, and user overrides. NEVER rewrites the model's output; the calling skill decides whether to use counter_prompt. Live since v0.0.2: the runtime evaluates sycophancy patterns deterministically via the heuristics in neurodock_mcp_guardrail.
x-clinical-review-required: Any change to this schema or to the heuristic implementation in packages/mcp-guardrail/src/neurodock_mcp_guardrail/heuristics/ requires sign-off from clinical-reviewer and consultation with the clinical advisory board per ETHICS.md commitment 3 and plan.md §8.

input.description: At least one of candidate_response or recent_user_messages MUST be provided (enforced via anyOf).

candidate_response.description: A draft model response to evaluate for over-validation patterns. Verbatim; the server does not paraphrase. Optional when recent_user_messages is provided (reassurance-seeking can be detected from the user side alone).

recent_user_messages.description: Recent user messages, oldest-first, scoped to the same decision_context. Used to detect repeated-reassurance-request patterns. Optional when candidate_response is provided.

decision_context.description: Optional. The decision under discussion (e.g. 'whether to ship the v0.2 RFC today'). Used to scope reassurance-seeking detection; without it, the server cannot determine 'same decision' and falls back to within-response detection only.

detected.description: True when a sycophancy pattern was matched above the confidence threshold.

pattern.description: Closed v0.1.0 vocabulary. 'unconditional_agreement' = the candidate_response agrees with the user without naming trade-offs on a subjective question; 'repeated_reassurance_request' = the user has asked for validation of the same decision_context >=3 times in recent_user_messages; 'praise_without_evidence' = the candidate_response opens with affirmation lacking referenced prior evidence; 'escalating_validation' = the candidate_response intensifies agreement compared to its own prior turns; 'other' = pattern matched but does not fit a canonical bucket; 'none' = no pattern. Each non-'none' value MUST be backed by a documented heuristic in packages/mcp-guardrail/src/neurodock_mcp_guardrail/heuristics/sycophancy.py.

confidence.description: Confidence on a 0..1 scale. Per ETHICS.md commitment 5, callers SHOULD NOT surface a counter_prompt when confidence < 0.5; below that threshold the most likely outcome is a false-positive that condescends to the user.

matched_spans.description: Verbatim text spans from candidate_response or recent_user_messages that matched the heuristic. Empty when detected is false. Surfaced so users auditing a detection can see exactly what the rule caught.

at.description: Timestamp when source is recent_user_messages; omitted for candidate_response spans.

counter_prompt.description: Optional. A short instruction the calling skill MAY prepend to a re-roll of the model response, asking the model to ground in prior evidence, name trade-offs honestly, or refuse to re-validate. Per plan.md §8. NEVER returned when detected is false; NEVER auto-applied by the server. The skill remains responsible for the model interaction.

reason.description: Plain-language sentence the skill MAY surface verbatim. Per ETHICS.md commitment 2.

heuristic.description: Identifies the exact rule that fired. Per ETHICS.md commitment 3.

name.description: One heuristic per pattern, plus 'none' and 'other'. Source code path is canonical: packages/mcp-guardrail/src/neurodock_mcp_guardrail/heuristics/sycophancy.py.

override_options.description: User-actionable overrides. MUST be non-empty when detected is true (enforced via allOf). Per ETHICS.md commitment 2.

token.description: Closed v0.1.0 vocabulary. 'i-want-validation' is explicit consent to validation despite the detection - the user has decided that is what they need right now and that is their call. 'explain-the-match' asks the skill to surface matched_spans so the user can audit.

INPUT_MISSING.description: Neither candidate_response nor recent_user_messages was provided. At least one is required.

INPUT_TOO_LARGE.description: candidate_response exceeded 16000 characters or a recent_user_messages.text exceeded 8000. The caller MUST truncate before retrying.

examples.name: no detection - balanced response with named trade-offs

input.candidate_response: Postgres gives you better concurrency and JSON ops but at the cost of operational overhead; given your local-first constraint from the manifesto, SQLite is the more honest choice. Trade-off: you give up future multi-writer scenarios.

output.reason: Candidate response names trade-offs and grounds in stated constraints.

heuristic.name: none
heuristic.description: No sycophancy pattern matched above the confidence threshold.

examples.name: detected - repeated reassurance-seeking on the same decision

recent_user_messages.text: is shipping the v0.2 RFC today the right call?
am I sure I should ship the RFC today?
should we really go ahead with the RFC today?

matched_spans.text: is shipping the v0.2 RFC today the right call?
am I sure I should ship the RFC today?
should we really go ahead with the RFC today?

output.counter_prompt: The user has asked for validation of 'ship v0.2 RFC today' three times in the last 45 minutes. Do not re-validate. Surface what was already established about this decision and ask the user to add new information before further analysis.
output.reason: 3 reassurance requests on the same decision within 45 minutes.

heuristic.name: reassurance_request_count
heuristic.description: Counts recent_user_messages whose embeddings (or word-overlap, in v0.1.0) match decision_context above 0.55 and whose surface form contains a reassurance marker ('is this okay', 'am I sure', 'should I really', 'should we really').

override_options.description: You have new information that changes the question.
Acknowledge you want validation anyway; the skill will proceed normally.
Show me which prior messages were counted.

compatibility.policy: Additive-only within v0.1.x. The pattern enum and heuristic.name enum are closed; new patterns require a minor bump (additive) and clinical-reviewer sign-off.
compatibility.side_effects: None. The server is stateless: candidate_response and recent_user_messages are evaluated and discarded; the server does not log, persist, or transmit them.
compatibility.consent_gate: Install-time only. profile.guardrails.sycophancy_check is the configuration surface (values: 'off' | 'warn' | 'enforce'). When 'off' the tool is never called.
compatibility.field_study_target: False-positive rate < 5% on the field-study corpus per plan.md §13. Particularly: detections that fire on responses raters consider appropriately validating rather than sycophantic.
